title: Basil Pesto
tagline: Fresh, bright, and takes 5 minutes. Once you make it yourself, you won't go back.
description: Classic Genovese pesto with fresh basil, garlic, pine nuts, parmesan, and olive oil. Toss with pasta, spread on sandwiches, or stir into soup.
ingredients: 1 Tbsp lemon juice (optional, keeps it green)
steps: Toast pine nuts in a dry skillet over medium heat 2-3 minutes until golden. Watch carefully—they burn fast.
Combine basil, toasted pine nuts, and garlic in a food processor. Pulse until coarsely chopped.
Add parmesan, salt, and pepper. Pulse to combine.
With the processor running, slowly drizzle in olive oil until smooth.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Add lemon juice if using.
Use immediately or store with a thin layer of olive oil on top to prevent browning.
notes: Pine nut swap: Walnuts, almonds, or cashews work. Pine nuts are traditional but expensive.
Keeping it green: A squeeze of lemon and blanching the basil (10 seconds in boiling water, then ice bath) help prevent browning.
Storage: Refrigerate up to 1 week with oil on top. Freeze in ice cube trays up to 3 months.
Pasta ratio: Use about 1/4 cup pesto per serving of pasta, loosened with pasta water.
Beyond pasta: Spread on sandwiches, mix into mayo, drizzle on pizza, stir into soups, or use as a marinade.
